# Rational Functions & Angular Measure

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this chapter, you will be able to:

- Identify asymptotes and domain restrictions of a rational function.
- Interpret the long-run behavior of a rational function in context.
- Convert between degrees and radians.
- Compute arc length.

---

## Rational Functions

*(Asymptotes, domain restrictions, behavior; concentration dilution models — asymptote as equilibrium concentration.)*

### Concept Check: Finding Asymptotes {-}

*(To be drafted.)*

## Angular Measure

*(Degrees, radians, arc length; converting wind-direction/lat-long angular data.)*

### Concept Check: Degree-Radian Conversion {-}

*(To be drafted.)*

## Practice: Dilution / Equilibrium Modeling

*(To be drafted.)*

## Skills Drills

### Simplifying Rational Expressions

*(To be drafted.)*

### Radian Conversions

*(To be drafted.)*
